content:
Key Findings

  • Lancaster City Council's Environmental Services department spent £34.0M in 2021/22 to 2025/26, accounting for 18.3% of total council expenditure.
  • The department spent more than any other council service, with Health & Housing in second place at £21.2M.
  • Environmental Services spending represented 18.3% of the council's total £185.7M expenditure over the five-year period.

Environmental Services: Lancaster's Largest Spending Department

Lancaster City Council's Environmental Services department stands out as the largest spending area, with £34.0M allocated between 2021/22 and 2025/26. This substantial investment represents 18.3% of the council's total expenditure of £185.7M during this period. The department's budget significantly outstrips other services, with the next highest spender, Health & Housing, receiving £21.2M (11.4% of total spend).

The scale of Environmental Services spending reflects its broad remit, which typically includes waste management, street cleaning, parks maintenance, and other public realm services. With such a significant portion of the council's resources directed towards this area, it plays a crucial role in maintaining Lancaster's quality of life and environmental standards.

Comparing Departmental Expenditure

To put Environmental Services spending in context, we can compare it with other major council departments. The table below shows the top ten spending areas, with Environmental Services clearly leading the way.

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 Environmental Services £34.0M 18.3%
2 Health & Housing £21.2M 11.4%
3 Resources £20.0M 10.8%
4 ENVIRONMENT AND PLACE £17.9M 9.6%
5 RESOURCES £16.0M 8.6%

While Environmental Services receives the largest share of the council's budget, other departments also command substantial resources. Health & Housing's £21.2M allocation, for instance, represents a significant investment in community well-being. The presence of multiple departments with similar names (e.g., Resources and RESOURCES) suggests potential areas for consolidation or clarification in council reporting.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Lancaster City Council about the specific services covered by the Environmental Services budget
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about how Environmental Services spending is allocated and monitored
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers are providing services to the Environmental Services department
  • Write to your local councillor about the balance of spending between Environmental Services and other council departments
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends across all Lancaster City Council departments
This article is based on Lancaster City Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
